mnemonics: MIND YOUR P'S AND Q'S: Pointers & Nodes, Questions about memory (malloc/free), Structures for data
keyPoints: A chain is represented using a linked list of nodes, where each node contains data and a pointer to the next node
Dynamic memory allocation (malloc) is used to create nodes at runtime, and free() is used to deallocate them to prevent memory leaks
The chain is accessed via a head pointer that points to the first node in the list, with the last node's pointer set to NULL to mark the end
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check if malloc() returned NULL (indicating memory allocation failure) before using the pointer
Not setting the 'next' pointer of the last node to NULL, which can cause infinite loops during traversal
Accessing node data through a pointer after calling free() on that node (dangling pointer reference), leading to undefined behavior
